Keep this terse. State what the PR does and the source you used. The diff covers the rest.
-->

## What

<!-- One or two sentences. e.g. "Add Boston, MA. Source: City of Boston Curbside Recycling page." -->

## Source

<!-- URL of the authoritative source. Required for any rule change. -->
- Primary source URL:
- Date verified (today, ISO):
- Wayback snapshot (recommended): <!-- https://web.archive.org/save/<URL> -->

## Verification level

- [ ] `unverified` — LLM-extracted or unchecked, awaiting community review
- [ ] `reviewed` — I read every rule against the source URL and confirmed it matches
- [ ] `resident_confirmed` — I live in this jurisdiction and have verified the rules in practice

## Conflict-of-interest disclosure (if applicable)

<!--
Are you employed by a waste hauler, recycling industry trade group, or a municipal solid-waste department?
If yes, disclose here. Sources must remain public-only — see CONTRIBUTING.md.
-->

## Pre-submit checks

- [ ] `binwise validate` passes locally with zero errors
- [ ] `binwise format --check` passes (files are canonical)
- [ ] All sources are public (`.gov`, hauler page, or other published page; not blog/forum/AI summary)
- [ ] No emojis in commit messages, file content, or this PR description
